commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs human PR handling because the contributor must add real after-fix provider proof; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to attempt.; Cleared: The diff only changes provider payload guard logic and a focused test; it does not touch dependencies, CI, secrets, install scripts, or other supply-chain surfaces. (sha=85a37fa8b5c0e749afc65dabe445bdacaec21610)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates DeepSeek V4 extra-parameter handling so Microsoft Foundry alias providers skip the unsupported top-level `thinking` payload and adds a regression test for one alias provider.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +20. Total +23 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still lets `microsoft-foundry-9433/deepseek-v4-pro` reach the DeepSeek thinking wrapper, and the linked issue provides live failing provider evidence. I did not execute a live Foundry repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human PR handling because the contributor must add real after-fix provider proof; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to attempt.

target.title: fix(agents): skip foundry aliases for deepseek thinking
target.body:
Fixes #90520.

## Summary

- Treat `microsoft-foundry-*` alias providers the same as the canonical `microsoft-foundry` provider for the DeepSeek V4 thinking-parameter guard.
- Add regression coverage for `microsoft-foundry-9433` so Foundry aliases do not receive the unsupported top-level `thinking` payload field.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `microsoft-foundry-9433/DeepSeek-V4-Pro` should not receive the DeepSeek V4 `thinking` parameter that Microsoft Foundry rejects, while preserving the existing OpenAI-compatible payload fields.

Real environment tested: macOS arm64 local OpenClaw checkout on current `origin/main` (`cb5bb9b9`), Node `v26.0.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner-extraparams.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner-extraparams.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-90520

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  132 passed (132)
   Start at  05:06:55
   Duration  3.11s (transform 2.24s, setup 194ms, import 2.77s, tests 68ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.75s
```

Observed result after fix: the new regression case exercises provider `microsoft-foundry-9433` with model `deepseek-v4-pro`; the payload keeps `reasoning_effort: "high"` and has no top-level `thinking` property.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Microsoft Foundry request from this checkout; verification is the focused source-level payload mutation path that was adding the rejected request field.
target.author: sahibzada-allahyar

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
